Name: LayerZero Labs
What they Do: LayerZero is an omnichain interoperability protocol enabling seamless communication and asset transfers across blockchains. They’re focused on simplifying cross-chain interactions, a core overlap with InclusiveLayer’s mission
Why: Their work on bridging fragmented blockchain ecosystems could leverage our SDK to simplify developer integration and provide a unified wallet interface for users across chains. Recent posts on X highlight their collaborations with chains like Aptos and Solana, indicating a need for accessible tools like ours

Name: Axelar Network
What they Do: Axelar provides a decentralized cross-chain infrastructure for dApps, enabling secure and scalable interoperability. They’re active in DeFi and cross-chain messaging
Why: Axelar’s focus on developer-friendly cross-chain solutions aligns with our SDK, which could enhance their offerings by simplifying wallet integrations and liquidity aggregation for their ecosystem of builders. Their X activity shows ongoing engagement with developers, a key target for us

Name: Wormhole
What they Do: Wormhole is a cross-chain messaging protocol connecting over 20 blockchains, widely used for NFT and token bridges
Why: Wormhole’s extensive chain support (EVM and non-EVM) matches our cross-chain capabilities. Their builders could use the SDK to streamline user onboarding and wallet management, reducing friction in cross-chain NFT or DeFi apps. Recent X posts emphasize their developer community

Name: SushiSwap
What they Do: SushiSwap is a multichain DEX (decentralized exchange) operating on chains like Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Aptos, focusing on swapping, liquidity provision, and DeFi tools
Why: SushiSwap’s multichain expansion could benefit from our liquidity aggregation and unified interface, making it easier for developers to build on their platform and for users to interact across wallets. Their X presence shows active updates on new chain integrations, signaling a need for simplified tooling

Name: BuildOnBase
What they Do: Base is an Ethereum L2 chain by Coinbase, designed for low-cost, scalable dApps. The community on X is full of builders creating consumer-focused apps like DeFi protocols and NFT marketplaces
Why: Base’s developer ecosystem could use our SDK to integrate with other chains and wallets effortlessly, enhancing cross-chain accessibility for their dApps. X posts from builders here often discuss scaling user adoption—our consumer chain focus could help

Name: Maverick Protocol
What they Do: Maverick Protocol is a DeFi project focused on efficient liquidity provision across chains like Ethereum and zkSync. @0xMav is an active contributor sharing updates on X
Why: Maverick’s cross-chain DeFi ambitions align with InclusiveLayer’s liquidity aggregation and developer tools. The SDK could simplify their wallet integrations and cross-chain liquidity sourcing, boosting their development pace

Name: Tomo
What they Do: Tomo is a social wallet project aiming to onboard non-crypto users to Web3 via simple interfaces, active on X with community updates
Why: Tomo’s focus on user-friendly blockchain access mirrors InclusiveLayer’s goal of onboarding developers and users. The SDK’s unified wallet support could enhance Tomo’s cross-chain social features, making it a strong candidate
